name: Banana-Date Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix nonfat milk and vinegar in a bowl until thickened.
2. Sift together flour, sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and salt into the mixing bowl.
3. Stir to blend.
4. Lightly beat eggs and egg white separately.
5. Add the beaten eggs to the flour mixture with mashed bananas, pureed dates, soured milk, vanilla, and nuts (if using).
6. Stir just until blended.
7. Pour batter into a greased 12-cup bundt pan.
8. Bake at 350°F for 45-55 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
9. Remove from oven and let cool on a wire rack.
10. If desired, sprinkle with powdered sugar before serving.
This recipe makes 16 servings. Note: You can substitute nonfat milk and vinegar with buttermilk if preferred. Pureed Dates replace 3/4 cup shortening. Each serving (without walnuts or powdered sugar) contains approximately:
- 212 calories
- 198 mg sodium
- 27 mg cholesterol
- 1 gram fat
- 48 grams carbohydrates
- 4 grams protein
- .33 gram fiber
- 4% calories from fat
Compared to a traditional Banana-Date Cake made with shortening and no dates, each serving (without walnuts) contains:
- 257 calories
- 197 mg sodium
- 27 mg cholesterol
- 9 grams fat
- 40 grams carbohydrates
- 4 grams protein
- .09 gram fiber
- 33% calories from fat
Optional walnuts per serving contain approximately:
- 24 calories
- .37 mg sodium
- 0 mg cholesterol
- 2.3 grams fat
- .75 gram carbohydrates
- .5 gram protein
- .17 gram fiber
